content.problemStatement:
You are given integers NNN and MMM.

Find the number of arrays AAA such that:


‚ą£A‚ą£=N|A| = N‚ą£A‚ą£=N
1‚Č§Ai‚Č§M1 \le A_i \le M1‚Č§Ai‚Äč‚Č§M for all iii (1‚Č§i‚Č§N)(1 \le i \le N)(1‚Č§i‚Č§N)
There exists an index iii (1‚Č§i‚Č§N‚ąí1)(1 \le i \le N - 1)(1‚Č§i‚Č§N‚ąí1) such that F(A,1,i)=F(A,i+1,N)F(A, 1, i) = F(A, i + 1, N)F(A,1,i)=F(A,i+1,N).

Here, F(A,l,r)F(A, l, r)F(A,l,r) denotes the number of distinct elements in [Al,Al+1,‚Ä¶Ar][A_l, A_{l + 1}, \ldots A_r][Al‚Äč,Al+1‚Äč,‚Ä¶Ar‚Äč]
Since the number might be too large, please find it modulo 998‚ÄČ244‚ÄČ353998\,244\,353998244353.


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
Each test case consists of a single line of input, containing two space-separated integers NNN and MMM.


Output Format
For each test case, output on a new line the number of valid arrays AAA modulo 998‚ÄČ244‚ÄČ353998\,244\,353998244353.
constraints: The sum of NNN over all test cases won't exceed 500050005000.
